| Mobile daily budget progress accessibility | `apps/mobile/src/features/budget/components/BudgetProgressBar.tsx`, `apps/mobile/src/features/budget/__tests__/budget.components.test.tsx` | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- budget.components.test.tsx --runInBand --testNamePattern "capped progress"` failed because overspent progress only announced `오늘 예산 사용률 120%`; GREEN same command PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- budget.components.test.tsx --runInBand`; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run format:check`; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test`; `corepack pnpm run format:check`; `git diff --check` | Daily budget progress now tells assistive technology users when the visual bar is capped at 100% while the real server-authoritative usage rate is over budget, preserving the overspend amount while avoiding contradictory progress feedback. |

## 2026-07-05 Mobile Root Bootstrap Safe Toast Guard

| Area                                   | Path                                                                                                 | Status        | Verification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| -------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Mobile root bootstrap failure toast UX | `apps/mobile/app/_layout.tsx`, `apps/mobile/src/shared/styles/__tests__/clean-fintech-theme.test.ts` | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- clean-fintech-theme.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "root bootstrap failure toasts"` failed because root bootstrap toasts could use raw exception messages; GREEN same command PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- clean-fintech-theme.test.ts --runInBand`; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run format:check`; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test`; `corepack pnpm run format:check`; `git diff --check` | Root bootstrap authentication-expired and offline-fallback failures now show fixed Korean user-facing copy through `safeBootstrapErrorMessage` instead of forwarding raw exception messages into app toasts. This protects app-start UX from leaked server URLs, tokens, or parser details while preserving offline fallback behavior. |

## 2026-07-05 Mobile Public Config Error Guard

| Area                                    | Path                                                                                                  | Status        | Verification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| --------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Mobile public app config error handling | `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/mobile-api.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/__tests__/mobile-api.test.ts` | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- mobile-api.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "redacts public app config network failures"` failed with raw fetch error exposure; GREEN same command PASS; RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- mobile-api.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "redacts malformed public app config JSON"` failed with raw JSON parser exposure; GREEN same command PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- mobile-api.test.ts --runInBand`; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run format:check` | Public `/api/v1/public/app-config` fetch failures are now converted to `PUBLIC_APP_CONFIG_NETWORK_ERROR`, and malformed JSON responses are converted to `PUBLIC_APP_CONFIG_INVALID_RESPONSE` before reaching UI flows, preventing raw request URLs, token-like network messages, parser messages, or copied response text from surfacing in the app. This is an app-code hardening slice, not production URL reachability proof. |

To avoid treating a developer PC as release proof, present env values remain unverified unless `SECRET_PROOF_STORE` or the collector option names an approved store such as GitHub Environments, GitHub Actions runtime, Cloudflare Worker secret, Neon, EAS secret store, or provider secret store. This adds the release evidence path but does not prove production secret configuration until a trusted environment runs it. |

## 2026-07-01 Git Remote HEAD Release Guard Update

| Area                          | Path                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Status        | Verification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ----------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Git remote HEAD release guard | `scripts/release/check-release-readiness.mjs`, `scripts/release/check-release-readiness.test.mjs`, `AGENTS.md`, `docs/codex/13_BASELINE_VERIFICATION_MAP.md`, `docs/codex/14_EXTERNAL_RELEASE_EVIDENCE.md` | Verified PASS | RED `node --test scripts/release/check-release-readiness.test.mjs --test-name-pattern "local release commit"`; GREEN `node --test scripts/release/check-release-readiness.test.mjs --test-name-pattern "local release commit"`; RED/GREEN `node --test scripts/release/check-release-readiness.test.mjs --test-name-pattern "local origin main tracking"`; `node --test scripts/release/check-release-readiness.test.mjs`; `corepack pnpm run test:root-scripts`; `corepack pnpm run check:external-integrations`; `corepack pnpm run check:release-readiness -- --soft`; `corepack pnpm run format:check` | Added a release-readiness guard that runs when authenticated Git push proof is claimed: it compares `git rev-parse HEAD` with `git ls-remote origin refs/heads/main` and blocks release readiness if the local release commit has not been pushed. When live remote reads are unavailable inside the local Node sandbox, it falls back to local `refs/remotes/origin/main` with a warning. This keeps the tracked external proof commit historical while preventing a stale local HEAD from being reported as release-ready. |

| ------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |

No raw financial data, token value, local file path, or store credential was added. |

## 2026-07-04 Mobile/API Upload Idempotency Update

| Area                                 | Path                                                                                                                                                                                                           | Status        | Verification                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| ------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

API direct upload handling now records idempotency keys internally, strips them from public responses, returns the same attachment on same-user safe retry, and rejects same-key/different-file conflicts. This improves runtime duplicate-upload behavior but does not replace R2, Cloudflare secret-binding, deployed smoke, or store-submit evidence gates. |

## 2026-07-02 Dependency Security Audit Lockfile Closure

| Area                                   | Path                                                                                                                                                     | Status        | Verification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| -------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Dependency audit vulnerability closure | `package.json`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, `release/security-audit-evidence.json`, `AGENTS.md`, `release/README.md`, `docs/codex/14_EXTERNAL_RELEASE_EVIDENCE.md` | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm audit --audit-level=high --prod=false --json` exited 1 with 2 low, 5 moderate, 0 high, and 0 critical vulnerabilities; GREEN after `corepack pnpm install --lockfile-only` and patched overrides for `diff`, `esbuild`, `js-yaml`, `ts-deepmerge`, and `uuid`; final audit exited 0 with 0 low, 0 moderate, 0 high, and 0 critical vulnerabilities; `node scripts\release\check-release-readiness.mjs --soft` reports `PASS security-audit:*` | Closed the dependency security audit release blocker for the current lockfile without committing raw audit JSON, advisories, dependency payloads, registry responses, tokens, or provider logs. No `node_modules/` directory was recreated; only the lockfile and no-secret evidence were refreshed. Release readiness remains BLOCKED by runtime secrets, Cloudflare runtime resources, DB migration/seed/smoke/rollback proof, public URL proof, mobile native build/E2E/store-submit proof, and missing `eas` on PATH. |

Additional validation note: `corepack pnpm run format:check` was attempted after the dependency cleanup and failed because local `node_modules/` is intentionally absent, so `prettier` is not available. This was treated as an environment/dependency-restoration blocker, not as a formatting failure; `git diff --cached --check` passed for the committed diff.

| Variable expense DB-backed repository contract | `services/api/src/repositories/variable-expenses.repository.ts`, `services/api/src/app.ts`, `services/api/src/index.ts`, `services/api/tests/mobile-variable-expense-contract.test.ts`, `services/api/tests/variable-expenses-db-repository.test.ts`, `docs/codex/01_PROJECT_BRIEF.md`, `docs/codex/07_API_CONTEXT.md` | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm exec vitest run tests/mobile-variable-expense-contract.test.ts tests/variable-expenses-db-repository.test.ts --passWithNoTests` failed on missing app repository injection header and missing DB repository module; GREEN targeted rerun; `corepack pnpm run typecheck:strict`: PASS; `corepack pnpm run test`: PASS, 8 files and 17 tests; `corepack pnpm run format:check`: PASS; `git diff --check`: PASS | Added a Neon/Postgres variable expense repository that maps mobile quick-add input to the existing `daily_budgets` and `variable_expenses` migration tables, including daily-budget upsert, idempotency lookup, KRW integer validation, category/status mapping, and owner-id redaction. The Worker selects this repository only when a supported database URL exists; otherwise the existing in-memory fallback remains. Real Neon migration/smoke/rollback proof is still a release gate. |

The screen keeps a disabled submit state until the draft is valid, sends anonymous comments through the service validator, parses the server response through `parseCommunityComment()`, appends the returned comment, increments the server detail count when available, clears the draft only after success, and shows a failure toast without pretending local publication succeeded. Deployed API auth, DB persistence, native E2E, and store-console proof remain separate release gates. |

Report payloads use the server-supported `ABUSE` reason type, avoid raw financial/personal data, and show success/failure toasts without pretending moderation succeeded locally. Deployed API auth, DB persistence, native E2E, and store-console proof remain separate release gates.                                                               |

The wrapper reads the stored access token via `MOBILE_ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY`, attaches only a valid bearer token, drops malformed token values, and keeps refresh tokens out of feature API request headers. Auth login/register remain unauthenticated entrypoints. Deployed API auth, DB user persistence, native E2E, and production smoke remain separate release gates.                                                                                     |
| Auth refresh/logout session flow    | `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/api.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/types.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/constants.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts`, `docs/codex/01_PROJECT_BRIEF.md`, `docs/codex/06_MOBILE_APP_CONTEXT.md`                                                                                                                                                                               | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts --runInBand` failed while `api.refresh` and `api.logout` were missing; second RED failed because rejected refresh did not clear the local access token; GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 1 suite and 7 tests; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck:strict`: PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run lint`: PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test`: PASS, 32 suites and 124 tests                                                     | Added `AuthApiClient.refresh()` for `POST /api/v1/auth/refresh` and `AuthApiClient.logout()` for `POST /api/v1/auth/logout`. Refresh relies on the server HttpOnly refresh cookie with `credentials: include`, stores only the rotated access token, never stores raw refresh tokens, and clears the local access token on rejected refresh. Logout calls server session revocation/cookie clearing and deletes only the local access token. Deployed API smoke, DB staging proof, native E2E, and store-console proof remain separate release gates.                                                                                 |
| MY logout server wiring             | `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/mobile-api.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/__tests__/mobile-api.test.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/shared/styles/clean-fintech-screens.tsx`, `apps/mobile/src/shared/styles/__tests__/clean-fintech-theme.test.ts`, `apps/mobile/app/(tabs)/profile/index.tsx`, `docs/codex/01_PROJECT_BRIEF.md`, `docs/codex/06_MOBILE_APP_CONTEXT.md`                                                                                      | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/shared/api/__tests__/mobile-api.test.ts --runInBand` failed because `createMobileAuthApi()` ignored the injected token store; RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/shared/styles/__tests__/clean-fintech-theme.test.ts --runInBand` failed because MY had no `profileAuthApi.logout`/`logoutSession` wiring; GREEN targeted reruns passed 1 suite/3 tests and 1 suite/21 tests; full mobile verification now reports 32 suites and 125 tests                                                                    | `createMobileAuthApi()` now uses an injected auth-capable token store when supplied and keeps SecureStore as the runtime default. The Clean Fintech MY screen now exposes a logout action that calls `POST /api/v1/auth/logout` through `AuthApiClient.logout()`, clears the local profile snapshot after server success, routes back to `/(auth)/login`, and keeps raw refresh tokens out of app storage. Native E2E/store proof and deployed API/DB smoke remain separate release gates.                                                                                                                                            |
| Logout local token cleanup          | `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/api.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts`, `docs/codex/01_PROJECT_BRIEF.md`, `docs/codex/05_PRIVACY_ADS_SECURITY.md`, `docs/codex/06_MOBILE_APP_CONTEXT.md`                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts --runInBand` failed because rejected server logout left the local access token undeleted; GREEN targeted rerun passed 1 suite and 8 tests; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`: PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test`: PASS, 32 suites and 129 tests; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run lint`: PASS                                                                                                                          | `AuthApiClient.logout()` now attempts local `MOBILE_ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY` deletion in a `finally` block so the app does not keep trusting a local bearer token after the user asks to log out, even when the server revoke request returns an error. The server logout request, HttpOnly cookie boundary, privacy headers, and no-refresh-token-storage rule remain intact. Native E2E/store proof and deployed API/DB smoke remain separate release gates.                                                                                                                                                                               |
| Feature API 401 refresh retry       | `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/mobile-api.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/__tests__/mobile-api.test.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/shared/styles/__tests__/clean-fintech-theme.test.ts`, `docs/codex/01_PROJECT_BRIEF.md`, `docs/codex/06_MOBILE_APP_CONTEXT.md`                                                                                                                                                                                             | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/shared/api/__tests__/mobile-api.test.ts --runInBand` failed because a protected feature API 401 immediately surfaced as login-required without calling refresh; GREEN targeted rerun passed 1 suite and 4 tests; full mobile verification now reports 32 suites and 126 tests                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Shared feature API fetchers now perform one `POST /api/v1/auth/refresh` attempt through the raw Auth API when a protected API returns 401, rely on the server HttpOnly refresh cookie, store only the rotated access token, and retry the original feature request once with the new bearer token. Failed refresh returns the original 401 to the feature parser to avoid loops or false success. Native E2E/store proof and deployed API/DB smoke remain separate release gates.                                                                                                                                                     |
| Feature API refresh single-flight   | `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/mobile-api.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/__tests__/mobile-api.test.ts`, `docs/codex/01_PROJECT_BRIEF.md`, `docs/codex/06_MOBILE_APP_CONTEXT.md`                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/shared/api/__tests__/mobile-api.test.ts --runInBand` failed because two concurrent protected API 401 responses triggered two refresh calls; GREEN targeted rerun passed 1 suite and 5 tests; full mobile verification now reports 32 suites and 127 tests                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Shared authenticated fetchers now keep one in-flight refresh promise per fetcher instance. Concurrent protected API 401 responses await the same refresh attempt, then retry their original requests individually, reducing duplicated refresh calls during startup or multi-tab hydration while preserving the one-retry/no-false-success behavior. Native E2E/store proof and deployed API/DB smoke remain separate release gates.                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| Root bootstrap auth refresh retry   | `apps/mobile/app/_layout.tsx`, `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/__tests__/app-screen-contract.test.ts`, `docs/codex/01_PROJECT_BRIEF.md`, `docs/codex/06_MOBILE_APP_CONTEXT.md`                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- app-screen-contract.test.ts` failed because root bootstrap did not reference `/api/v1/auth/refresh`; GREEN targeted rerun passed 1 suite and 4 tests; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`: PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test`: PASS, 32 suites and 128 tests; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run lint`: PASS; `corepack pnpm run format:check`: PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run export:web`: PASS; `corepack pnpm run build`: PASS, 12 tasks | Root `/api/v1/mobile/bootstrap` now retries once after a 401 by using the existing Auth API refresh client, the server HttpOnly refresh cookie, and SecureStore access-token storage through `MOBILE_ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY`. Privacy headers, platform headers, correlation IDs, server-authoritative bootstrap behavior, and offline fallback remain intact. Native E2E/store proof, deployed API/DB smoke, runtime secrets, Cloudflare runtime evidence, and public URL proof remain separate release gates.                                                                                                                             |

Salary Home now hydrates today's variable expense list from the server before offline fallback, lets the user correct a selected server expense using the amount input, deletes selected server expenses, refreshes the daily budget after mutations, and does not show static examples when the server confirms an empty list. Native E2E, deployed API smoke, staging DB smoke, and store proof remain release gates. |

The community write screen now lets users pick image/PDF attachments, reads local bytes, uploads through the authenticated uploads API with privacy headers and server file policy alignment, shows uploaded attachment metadata, then attaches uploaded IDs to the server-created community post. Raw local file URI/path, auth tokens, salary, expense, savings, account, card, and push-token values are not included in the post payload. Deployed upload API smoke, native device E2E, and store proof remain release gates. |

GREEN checks: `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run build:e2e:android:local-debug:preflight`: PASS; `node --test apps/mobile/scripts/expo-local-android-debug-build.test.mjs apps/mobile/scripts/eas-local-android-build.test.mjs scripts/dev/clean-generated-junk.test.mjs`: PASS, 13 tests; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/config/__tests__/mobile-e2e-contract.test.ts --runInBand`: PASS, 4 tests; `corepack pnpm run check:package-manager-scripts`: PASS; `corepack pnpm run check:external-integrations`: PASS; `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`: PASS; `corepack pnpm run format:check`: PASS | Added a guarded no-EAS local Android debug build wrapper that checks workspace-local Expo CLI, Java/JBR, Android SDK tools, writes `android/local.properties`, injects required generated Android dependency/resource fixes, patches stale RN autolinking output, repairs Reanimated CMake directories, copies a verified APK header to `apps/mobile/build/e2e/android/salary-hijacking-e2e.apk`, and is exposed through mobile package scripts and Detox guidance. Actual APK generation is still blocked on this Windows/pnpm path by Expo native CMake `build.ninja` instability under the long real `.pnpm` package path; using `C:\shp` as a junction did not resolve it because Gradle still observed the real workspace path. This does not prove Android production AAB, native E2E, or store submission. Next safe routes are WSL/CI, a real short-path worktree/install, or EAS with verified `EXPO_TOKEN` evidence. |

GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 59 tests in the Clean Fintech contract suite. | Notification archive and delete actions still give fast local feedback, but if the server mutation fails the app restores the removed notification and unread count before showing the failure message. This prevents the app from leaving a local-success state for an unpersisted notification mutation and keeps notification state aligned with the server as the authority. |

| --------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 60 tests in the Clean Fintech contract suite. | Notification tap/open still performs fast local mark-read before safe deeplink navigation, but if `NotificationsApiClient.markRead()` fails the app restores the original notification status and unread count. This prevents a failed server mutation from being presented as persisted read state. |

If the server mutation fails, the app restores the exact prior list and unread count, preventing unpersisted read-all state from being shown as successful. |

## 2026-07-04 Variable Expense Receipt Attach Failure Handling

| Area                                                   | Path                                                                                                                                                                     | Status        | Verification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| ------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Mobile salary home receipt attach after expense create | `apps/mobile/src/shared/styles/clean-fintech-screens.tsx`, `apps/mobile/src/shared/styles/__tests__/clean-fintech-theme.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md` | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/shared/styles/__tests__/clean-fintech-theme.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "server-created expenses"` failed because a server-created variable expense could still fall through the broad catch and add an offline preview when receipt attachment failed. GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 62 tests in the Clean Fintech contract suite. | Split server-authoritative expense creation from the optional receipt attach step. If the expense is created but receipt attachment fails, the app keeps the server-created expense, clears the stale receipt attachment, refreshes server budget/expense snapshots, and tells the user to attach the receipt again instead of adding a duplicate offline preview expense. |

GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 63 tests in the Clean Fintech contract suite. | Split community post creation from optional attachment linking. If the post is created but uploaded attachments fail to link, the app clears the submitted draft/attachment queue, keeps the server-created post as the successful source of truth, and tells the user only the attachment connection failed instead of reporting the whole post creation as failed. |

## 2026-07-04 Mobile Auth Access Token Storage Guard

| Area                                          | Path                                                                                                                                                  | Status        | Verification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| --------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Mobile auth access token storage guard        | `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/auth-response.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md`     | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "malformed access tokens"` failed because a CR/LF-bearing access token was accepted and could be written to secure storage. GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 12 tests in the auth API suite. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test`: PASS, 33 suites and 201 tests. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`: PASS. `corepack pnpm run format:check`: PASS. `git diff --check`: PASS. | Login and signup auth response normalization now validates server-issued access tokens before the mobile app can persist them. Tokens longer than 8192 characters or containing CR/LF/tab characters are rejected as invalid auth responses, so malformed bearer material is not written into the platform token store. Refresh tokens and delivery/reset secrets remain server/cookie-bound and are not persisted by the mobile app. |
| Mobile bearer-prefixed token storage guard    | `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/auth-response.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md`     | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "bearer-prefixed"` failed because `Bearer server-token` was accepted and could be written to secure storage. GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 13 tests in the auth API suite. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`: PASS.                                                                                                                                                                             | Access tokens with any whitespace are now rejected before mobile persistence. This prevents a server or proxy response that includes an Authorization header value such as `Bearer ...` from being stored as the raw token and later rewrapped into an invalid or unsafe bearer header.                                                                                                                                               |
| Mobile authorization header token guard       | `apps/mobile/src/shared/storage/auth-token.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/shared/storage/__tests__/auth-token.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md` | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/shared/storage/__tests__/auth-token.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "bearer-prefixed"` failed because a bearer-prefixed value already present in secure storage still produced an `authorization` header. GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 3 tests in the auth token storage helper suite. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`: PASS.                                                                                                                                 | Stored mobile access tokens are now rejected at request-header attachment time when they contain any whitespace. This defense-in-depth guard prevents stale or externally corrupted secure-storage values such as `Bearer access.jwt.token` from being sent as `Bearer Bearer access.jwt.token`, while preserving valid compact access tokens.                                                                                        |
| Mobile whitespace-padded token response guard | `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/auth-response.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md`     | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "whitespace-padded"` failed because a server access token with leading/trailing spaces was silently trimmed and could be stored. GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 14 tests in the auth API suite. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`: PASS.                                                                                                                                                         | Auth response normalization now validates access tokens from the raw server string instead of the generic trimmed text helper. This prevents the mobile app from silently rewriting malformed server-issued token material before persistence, while preserving trim behavior for non-token fields such as user identifiers and status text.                                                                                          |
| Mobile non-positive token TTL guard           | `apps/mobile/src/shared/api/auth-response.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md`     | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/features/auth/__tests__/auth.api.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "non-positive access token TTLs"` failed because `accessTokenExpiresIn: 0` was silently converted into a 1-second persisted session. GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 15 tests in the auth API suite. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`: PASS.                                                                                                                                                     | Auth response normalization now rejects explicitly non-positive access token TTLs instead of clamping them to one second. This prevents login/refresh from persisting immediately expired or contract-invalid bearer tokens while keeping the default 900-second fallback for responses that omit TTL fields.                                                                                                                         |

| ---------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 7 tests. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`: PASS.   | Community validation now treats bare JWT-shaped strings as sensitive personal/auth data. This prevents users from accidentally publishing raw bearer/session material even when the text does not include obvious words such as token, bearer, refresh, push, or fcm.                                                   |
| Mobile community raw JWT redaction guard | `apps/mobile/src/features/community/community.redaction.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/community/__tests__/community.redaction.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md`  | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/features/community/__tests__/community.redaction.test.ts --runInBand` failed because a raw JWT-like token remained in `redactCommunityText()` output. GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 3 tests. Broader verification is recorded in the final task report.                                                     | Community redaction now masks bare JWT-shaped auth material with `[auth-redacted]` before applying the existing email, phone, resident ID, card, account, raw KRW, and keyword-token redactions. This keeps service/error/preview paths that use redaction from echoing raw bearer/session material back to the app UI. |
| Mobile community report reason redaction | `apps/mobile/src/features/community/community.service.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/community/__tests__/community.service.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md`      | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/features/community/__tests__/community.service.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "redacts sensitive report reason"` failed because report reasons sent raw phone/account/JWT text to the API payload. GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 4 tests. Broader verification is recorded in the final task report. | Community report payloads now redact sensitive user-entered reason text before calling `reportPost()` or `reportComment()`. This preserves the moderation workflow while preventing raw phone, account, token, JWT, KRW, or identifier strings from being copied into mobile API transport payloads.                    |
| Mobile upload sensitive file-name guard  | `apps/mobile/src/features/uploads/api.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/features/uploads/__tests__/uploads.api.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md`                              | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/features/uploads/__tests__/uploads.api.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "blocks sensitive Korean"` failed because sensitive Korean/identifier/token-like file names reached `Request` creation as a generic TypeError instead of a domain upload error. GREEN targeted rerun: PASS, 5 tests.         | Direct community attachment and variable-expense receipt uploads now reject file names containing Korean finance/privacy keywords, phone/account-like identifiers, or JWT-shaped tokens before network access. This prevents raw sensitive labels from being copied into the `x-upload-file-name` header.               |

RED `node --test scripts/release/check-release-readiness.test.mjs --test-name-pattern "Android is the primary"` failed because iOS evidence was still a hard blocker for the Google Play-first launch target. GREEN targeted reruns: PASS. `corepack pnpm run release:secrets-evidence`: PASS. `node scripts\release\check-release-readiness.mjs --soft --json`: reporting command PASS with `176 PASS / 28 BLOCKED / 3 WARN = 85.0%`. | `NEON_PROJECT_ID`, `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID`, `CF_ADMIN_WORKER_NAME`, `GITHUB_TOKEN`, and `GITHUB_REPOSITORY` now use no-secret external/runtime evidence instead of requiring local raw values. `DATABASE_URL`, `STAGING_DATABASE_URL`, `NEON_API_KEY`, `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN`, `EXPO_TOKEN`, `EAS_PROJECT_ID`, `SENTRY_DSN`, and `SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL` remain blocked until approved secret-store proof exists. The primary app launch target is now explicitly Android/Google Play, with iOS build/App Store proof tracked as post-launch evidence rather than claiming iOS is complete. |

The mobile client returns only provider/state/challenge/redirect/authorization URL fields and intentionally drops server `codeVerifier` material instead of persisting or exposing PKCE secrets. `expo-web-browser` opens a provider session only when the server returns an HTTPS `authorizationUrl`; deployed provider credentials and callback completion remain separate release gates. |

## 2026-07-04 Mobile Social OAuth Callback Completion Guard

RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test -- src/shared/styles/__tests__/clean-fintech-theme.test.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern "OAuth callback"` failed because `app/auth/oauth/callback.tsx` did not exist. GREEN targeted reruns: PASS. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run test`: PASS, 33 suites and 221 tests. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run typecheck`: PASS. `corepack pnpm run format:check`: PASS. | Mobile OAuth start now stores the server-issued PKCE verifier in SecureStore-compatible storage under a state-scoped key without exposing it in the returned result. The new callback route reads `code` and `state`, calls `AuthApiClient.completeOAuth()` against `POST /api/v1/auth/oauth/callback`, persists only the access token, clears the one-time verifier, and routes authenticated users to `/salary` while failed callbacks return to login. Provider production credentials and deployed callback smoke remain separate release gates. |

OAuth start now emits provider `authorizationUrl` values when a public provider client id is configured, including PKCE challenge parameters, without requiring or exposing provider client secrets. |

Broader verification in the task report includes full API/mobile tests, typecheck, format, diff, release readiness, and junk cleanup. | Mobile OAuth start now generates the PKCE verifier/challenge inside the app with `expo-crypto`, sends only the challenge to `GET /api/v1/auth/oauth`, stores only the local verifier under the returned state, and never depends on a verifier in the server response. The API accepts client-generated S256 challenges, stores a prefixed verification value for callback validation, omits `codeVerifier` from client-challenge start responses, and keeps the legacy server-generated verifier path compatible for older callers. |

The server returns enumeration-safe `accepted: true`, stores a fresh token only for existing users, emits a security event, and does not echo the raw email. The mobile client strips delivery-only tokens and stores no verification token. |

## 2026-07-04 Mobile Root Offline Gate Guard

| Area                              | Path                                                                                                                                      | Status        | Verification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| --------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

GREEN targeted rerun passed with 12 app screen contract tests. Full mobile verification passed with 34 suites and 241 tests. | Root bootstrap fallback now routes cached sessions through `offlineStatusFromCachedSession()`: unverified users stay behind `VERIFY_EMAIL`, onboarding-incomplete users stay behind `ONBOARDING`, MFA-required sessions return to auth, and only fully ready cached sessions can render the offline app shell. |

GREEN `corepack pnpm run format:check` PASS. | GitHub Actions now rely on the root `packageManager` declaration as the single pnpm version source and the external integration preflight rejects future duplicated `with.version` settings in `pnpm/action-setup`, preventing CI/release/security workflows from failing before install. |

## 2026-07-04 API Upload Idempotency Checksum Conflict Guard

| Area                                      | Path                                                                                                                                      | Status        | Verification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| ----------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Direct upload idempotency replay conflict | `services/api/src/routes/uploads.routes.ts`, `services/api/tests/mobile-uploads-contract.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md` | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/api test -- mobile-uploads-contract.test.ts` failed because same-key direct upload retries with different bytes returned the original upload as 200. GREEN rerun PASS with 26 files and 75 tests. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/api typecheck` PASS. `git diff --check` PASS. | `/api/v1/uploads/direct` now compares the computed SHA-256 checksum during idempotent replay checks, rejects same-key retries whose file bytes differ with `UPLOAD_IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT`, and keeps the conflict response free of raw idempotency keys, user identifiers, financial markers, and token markers. This does not prove deployed R2/Cloudflare runtime upload gates. |

## 2026-07-04 API Upload R2 Runtime Adapter

| Area                        | Path                                                                                                                                      | Status        | Verification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| --------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Direct upload R2 put/delete | `services/api/src/routes/uploads.routes.ts`, `services/api/tests/mobile-uploads-contract.test.ts`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md` | Verified PASS | RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/api test -- mobile-uploads-contract.test.ts` failed because a runtime `UPLOADS_BUCKET` binding did not receive uploaded bytes. GREEN rerun passed with 26 files and 77 tests after adding R2 `put` and delete handling. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/api typecheck` PASS. | `/api/v1/uploads/direct` now writes upload bytes to the Cloudflare `UPLOADS_BUCKET` binding when present, falls back to in-memory blobs for local/no-R2 tests, deletes the R2 object when the attachment is deleted, and keeps storage keys/R2 URLs out of API responses. This code path still requires real Cloudflare R2 bucket provisioning and runtime binding evidence before the R2 release gate can pass. |

GREEN targeted rerun PASS with 2 tests. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/api exec vitest run tests/variable-expenses-db-repository.test.ts tests/mobile-variable-expense-contract.test.ts --passWithNoTests`: PASS, 2 files and 4 tests. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/api run typecheck:strict`: PASS. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/api run test`: PASS, 27 files and 88 tests. `corepack pnpm run db:validate`: PASS. | Added an additive DB migration for `refund_amount` and `last_refund_idempotency_key`, updated server-authoritative daily-budget/payroll recalculation SQL to use active variable expense net amount after partial refunds, and updated the Neon variable expense repository response mapping so mobile/API receives correct refund and net spend values without exposing owner identifiers. Real Neon migration execution and staging smoke remain release gates. |

| Area                                     | Path                                                                                                                                                            | Status                                         | Verification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |

| Mobile Android E2E local build hardening | `apps/mobile/scripts/expo-local-android-debug-build.mjs`, `apps/mobile/scripts/expo-local-android-debug-build.test.mjs`, `docs/codex/08_FILE_COMPLETION_LOG.md` | Verified partial PASS; APK build still BLOCKED | RED `node --test apps\mobile\scripts\expo-local-android-debug-build.test.mjs` failed because the wrapper did not run `:expo-modules-core:configureCMakeDebug[x86_64]` before `assembleDebug`, did not retry once after a Windows CMake configure failure, and did not create object-output parent directories from `build.ninja`. GREEN rerun PASS with 7 tests. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile typecheck`: PASS. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile format:check`: PASS. `git diff --check`: PASS. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/mobile run build:e2e:android:local-debug`: FAIL. | The local Android E2E wrapper now explicitly configures `expo-modules-core`, repairs CMake output directories, retries that configure once on Windows, and parses `build.ninja` object outputs to create deeper parent directories. The actual APK gate remains blocked on this Windows machine by `ninja: error: manifest 'build.ninja' still dirty after 100 tries` in `expo-modules-core`; further attempts should move to WSL/CI/EAS native build evidence or a shorter real checkout path instead of stacking more wrapper retries. Generated Android build/cache artifacts were cleaned after verification-heavy runs. |

RED `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/api exec vitest run tests/mobile-uploads-contract.test.ts --testNamePattern "download URL is requested before scan completion"` failed because `/download` returned mojibake for not-yet-available attachments. GREEN `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/api exec vitest run tests/mobile-uploads-contract.test.ts`: PASS, 12 tests. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/api run typecheck`: PASS. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/api run format:check`: PASS. `corepack pnpm --filter @salary-hijacking/api test`: PASS, 27 files / 92 tests. `node scripts\release\check-release-readiness.mjs --soft`: BLOCKED, 85.5%. | Upload download/content failure responses now return readable Korean messages for unavailable attachments and missing storage objects, and the contract tests guard against common mojibake markers while keeping storage keys, user IDs, token markers, and raw financial identifiers out of response bodies. This improves real mobile attachment UX but does not remove external release blockers such as runtime secrets, Cloudflare R2/domain/TLS, staging smoke, or public URL proof. |

## Update Rule

When completing a work slice, append or update a row with:

- path,
- status,
- exact command,
- PASS/FAIL,
- remaining blockers.
